factual-accuracy.requirement: States 15,000 annual production hours exceeds physical maximum of 8,760 hours/year and clarifies modeling assumptions
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly notes that 15,000 annual hours exceeds the physical maximum of 8,760 hours/year and explains this is likely due to misinterpretation or aggregation across multiple machines, then provides realistic utilization assumptions (70–80% or ~6,000–7,000 productive hours/year).

factual-accuracy.requirement: Recommends coated carbide (TiAlN, AlTiN, or similar coatings) as primary or standard tool material for Ti-6Al-4V
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly recommends coated carbide tools as the industry standard for titanium machining, naming TiAlN coatings (and similar) under “Recommended Tooling Practices.” This positions coated carbide as the primary/standard tool material for Ti-6Al-4V.
